title: bundle@1
description: Repo-context bundle: a packed-text representation of a repo (e.g. emitted by Repomix, gitingest, codebase-digest). Unlike graph formats there are no nodes/edges — just packed content + a manifest pointing at it. The bundle JSON itself is small; the actual packed body lives at `content_url` so registry consumers can stream the raw markdown/xml/plaintext.

format.description: Optional self-identifier; the registry already knows the format from the entry, but producers commonly stamp it.

manifest.description: Producer-side metadata. Required so consumers can sniff tool identity, version, freshness, and the content body encoding without fetching it.

tool.description: Identifier of the producing tool, e.g. "repomix", "gitingest", "codebase-digest". Free-form so future packers can self-identify without a schema bump.

commit.description: 40-hex source-repo HEAD sha at generation time. Optional but strongly recommended — used by the registry drift detection.

byte_count.description: Total size in bytes of the packed body at content_url.

token_estimate.description: Approximate token count of the packed body. Estimator is producer-defined; consumers SHOULD treat as a hint only.

format.description: Encoding of the packed body at content_url. Lets consumers pick a parser without sniffing.

content_url.description: Raw URL of the packed text/xml/markdown body. Mandatory — without it there is no bundle.

summary.description: Optional one-paragraph plain-text summary of what is in the bundle.

files.description: Optional per-file manifest. Producers MAY omit when the file list would balloon the registry pointer; in that case file_count still gives a count.
